wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Emerald Pearland Events booking system project overview. Explain the system's purpose as a premium luxury event booking platform, its target audience (Nairobi-based luxury event clients), and core value proposition. Document the complete technology stack including Node.js/Express backend, MongoDB database, Brevo email services, Twilio WhatsApp integration, and modern frontend architecture. Describe the system's key features including automated booking workflows, admin dashboard management, real-time notifications, and mobile-responsive design. Include architectural overview showing the relationship between frontend website, admin portal, and backend API services. Provide business context explaining how this system streamlines luxury event coordination for Emerald Pearland Events. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of the booking journey from inquiry to completion.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend architecture documentation for the Emerald system. Detail the Express.js application structure including server initialization, middleware stack configuration, and route organization. Document the layered architecture showing how requests flow from incoming HTTP requests through authentication middleware to controller handlers and database operations. Explain the MVC pattern implementation with clear separation between models (Mongoose schemas), views (HTML templates), and controllers (route handlers). Detail the service layer architecture including email service, notification service, and cron service implementations. Document the middleware stack including admin authentication, CORS configuration, rate limiting, and security headers. Include request-response flow diagrams showing typical booking workflows and admin operations. Address error handling strategies, logging mechanisms, and performance optimization techniques. Explain the clustering architecture using PM2 for production deployment and load balancing across multiple instances.

wiki_catalogs.name: Client Booking APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for client-facing booking endpoints. Document the main booking submission endpoint POST /api/book-event including request validation rules, input sanitization, customer creation/update logic, and response formats. Detail the booking retrieval endpoint GET /api/book-event/:bookingId for status checking. Document the public gallery endpoint GET /api/gallery for accessing event photos. For each endpoint, specify request/response schemas, validation requirements, error handling, and security measures. Include practical examples of booking submission with proper data formatting, validation error responses, and successful booking confirmation. Document rate limiting mechanisms, input sanitization against XSS attacks, and the complete booking workflow from inquiry to confirmation. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common validation failures and integration examples with client applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Business Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the core business entities of Emerald Pearland Events. Detail the Booking model with its complete schema including event types (Wedding, Anniversary, Birthday Party, etc.), customer relationships, staff assignments, payment tracking, and status management. Document the Customer model covering personal information, contact details, and preference management. Explain the Staff model with role-based access, availability tracking, and assignment capabilities. Include field definitions, validation rules, enum constraints, and business logic. Document the many-to-many relationship between Bookings and Staff through the assignedStaff array, and the one-to-many relationship with Customers. Provide indexing strategies for performance optimization, query patterns for common business operations, and data lifecycle management. Include sample data structures and practical examples demonstrating booking creation, staff assignment, and customer management workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Automation Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Brevo (formerly Sendinblue) email automation service. Document the seven pre-built email templates including booking confirmation, follow-up messages, 48-hour reminders, staff assignment notifications, client feedback requests, and administrative alerts. Explain the Brevo API integration setup, template management, and delivery tracking mechanisms. Detail the email workflow orchestration including conditional logic for different booking statuses, recipient segmentation, and automated scheduling. Document the template customization process, dynamic content injection, and brand consistency requirements. Include configuration examples for Brevo API keys, sender domains, and template IDs. Provide troubleshooting guides for common delivery issues, bounce handling, and spam filtering. Address email analytics tracking, unsubscribe management, and GDPR compliance considerations. Include practical examples of email automation workflows showing the complete booking lifecycle from initial inquiry to post-event follow-up.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the admin authentication and security system. Document the JWT-based authentication mechanism including token generation, validation, and refresh processes. Explain the admin user model with password hashing, role-based access control, and session management. Detail the login workflow from credential verification to token issuance, including security measures like rate limiting and IP tracking. Document the admin middleware that protects routes, validates tokens, and enforces authorization levels. Include security best practices for token storage, transmission, and rotation. Provide troubleshooting guides for common authentication issues, token expiration problems, and failed login attempts. Address audit logging, security headers implementation, and protection against common attacks like CSRF and XSS.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for setting up the Emerald Pearland Events booking system. Document prerequisites including Node.js >=14.0.0, npm >=6.0.0, and MongoDB installation. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including dependency installation via npm install, environment configuration using .env.example template, and initial database setup. Explain the difference between development (npm run dev) and production (npm start) modes. Document essential environment variables including database connection strings, Brevo API keys, Twilio credentials, and JWT secret configuration. Include first-time setup procedures for admin user creation, email service configuration, and push notification setup. Provide verification steps to ensure all components are working correctly, including health check endpoints and basic booking functionality testing. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting tips for new deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend architecture documentation for the Emerald website and admin dashboard. Document the responsive design architecture with mobile-first approach and progressive web app features. Explain the frontend structure including main website pages (index.html, booking.html) and admin dashboard components. Detail the mobile optimization implementation including CSS injection, JavaScript adaptation, and touch interface design. Document the admin interface architecture with sidebar navigation, real-time updates, and interactive elements. Explain the static asset management including CSS, JavaScript, and media files organization. Detail the frontend-backend integration patterns including API consumption, form handling, and real-time communication. Document the user experience architecture focusing on luxury event booking flow and admin workflow efficiency. Include performance optimization strategies such as resource loading, caching, and progressive enhancement techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Management APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for admin-only management endpoints. Document the admin authentication system including JWT token generation, validation, and refresh mechanisms. Detail booking management endpoints including GET /api/admin/bookings for listing all bookings with filtering options, PATCH /api/admin/booking/:id/status for updating booking statuses, and individual booking retrieval endpoints. Document staff management APIs for assigning staff members, managing staff availability, and tracking staff performance. Include analytics endpoints for revenue reporting, booking statistics, and performance metrics. Document admin user management including user creation, permission management, and session handling. For each endpoint, specify authentication requirements, authorization levels, request/response schemas, and error codes. Provide examples of admin dashboard integrations, bulk operations, and administrative workflows. Include security considerations, rate limiting for admin endpoints, and troubleshooting guidance for authentication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative & Support Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for administrative and support entities in the Emerald system. Document the Admin model with authentication credentials, role-based permissions, and access control mechanisms. Explain the AdminSettings model covering system configuration, operational parameters, and business rules. Detail the AdminNotification model for notification management, alert systems, and communication tracking. Document the Analytics model with business metrics, performance tracking, and reporting data structures. Include field definitions, validation rules, and security considerations for each administrative model. Explain relationships with other entities, particularly how Admins interact with Bookings and Customer data. Document data retention policies, audit trail requirements, and compliance considerations. Provide examples of administrative workflows, system configuration scenarios, and analytics reporting patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Automated Workflow System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the automated workflow system powered by node-cron. Document the scheduled tasks including 48-hour event reminder emails, post-event feedback collection, staff assignment notifications, and administrative follow-ups. Explain the cron job configuration syntax, timing precision, and timezone handling. Detail the workflow orchestration logic including conditional triggers based on booking status, customer preferences, and event proximity. Document the integration with the email service for automated message delivery and the database operations for status updates. Include examples of workflow scenarios such as late booking follow-ups, no-show handling, and customer retention campaigns. Address error handling, retry mechanisms, and logging for failed workflows. Provide configuration guidelines for development vs production environments, including testing strategies for scheduled tasks. Document monitoring approaches for workflow execution tracking and performance optimization techniques for high-volume operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Booking Management Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the admin booking management interface. Document the real-time booking dashboard showing pending, confirmed, and completed bookings with filtering and sorting capabilities. Explain the booking creation workflow through the new booking form, including event details, client information, and pricing calculations. Detail the booking status management system with state transitions, update notifications, and history tracking. Document the client communication tools integrated with the booking interface, including email templates and WhatsApp messaging shortcuts. Include the booking search and filter functionality, bulk operations, and export capabilities. Provide user guides for common admin workflows like booking approval, rescheduling, cancellation processing, and client follow-up coordination. Address booking analytics integration and performance monitoring within the management interface.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Emerald Pearland Events system. Describe the high-level layered architecture including presentation layer (frontend website and admin dashboard), business logic layer (Express routes and controllers), data access layer (Mongoose models), and persistence layer (MongoDB). Document the MVC pattern implementation with clear separation of concerns between models, views, and controllers. Explain the component interactions showing data flow from client requests through authentication middleware to database operations. Detail the security architecture including JWT-based admin authentication, input sanitization, rate limiting, and CORS configuration. Document the real-time communication architecture including push notification system and WhatsApp integration. Include system context diagrams showing relationships with external services like Brevo and Twilio. Address scalability considerations, load balancing with PM2 clustering, and deployment topology. Explain cross-cutting concerns including logging, monitoring, and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data flow architecture documentation for the Emerald system. Detail the end-to-end data flow from client booking submissions through database persistence to automated communication workflows. Document the booking lifecycle data flow including initial inquiry capture, customer profile creation, staff assignment processes, and status tracking updates. Explain the real-time data flow patterns including push notifications, WhatsApp messaging, and email automation sequences. Detail the inter-service data flow between email service, notification service, and database operations. Document the data transformation patterns including booking validation, customer enrichment, and analytics data collection. Include sequence diagrams showing typical booking workflows and administrative processes. Address data consistency strategies, transaction handling, and error recovery mechanisms. Explain the caching strategies and data synchronization patterns used throughout the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content & Media Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for content and media management entities. Document the Testimonial model with client feedback structures, rating systems, approval workflows, and display configurations. Explain the Gallery model covering photo management, categorization, ordering systems, and media metadata. Detail field definitions, validation rules, and content moderation requirements for each model. Document relationships with Booking and Customer entities for testimonial verification and gallery photo attribution. Include indexing strategies for content retrieval, search optimization, and media processing workflows. Explain content lifecycle management including approval processes, archival procedures, and version control. Provide examples of testimonial submission workflows, gallery photo upload processes, and content moderation scenarios. Address performance considerations for media storage, CDN integration, and image optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Notifications
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the real-time notification system using web-push protocol. Document the service worker implementation including push subscription management, notification delivery, and browser compatibility handling. Explain the VAPID key configuration, encryption setup, and secure message transmission. Detail the admin dashboard notification system including subscription registration, notification triggering, and user permission management. Document the notification payload structure, custom styling options, and interactive notification features. Include setup instructions for development and production environments, including certificate management and service worker registration. Address browser-specific considerations, fallback mechanisms for unsupported browsers, and user preference handling. Provide examples of notification scenarios such as new booking alerts, status changes, and staff assignments. Document monitoring approaches for notification delivery rates, user engagement tracking, and troubleshooting common delivery issues. Include security considerations for push subscription management and protection against abuse.

wiki_catalogs.name: Staff Coordination System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the staff coordination and management system. Document the staff profile management including personnel registration, role assignments, skill categories, and availability tracking. Explain the staff scheduling interface with calendar integration, shift management, and conflict detection. Detail the staff notification system including push notifications, email alerts, and WhatsApp messages for booking assignments and reminders. Document the staff performance metrics, rating systems, and evaluation tracking. Include the staff communication tools within the admin interface, supervisor assignment workflows, and team coordination features. Provide guidelines for staff onboarding, training management, and professional development tracking. Address staff availability management, leave tracking, and workload balancing across the team.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Emerald Pearland Events backend RESTful APIs. Document all public endpoints including client booking submission (/api/bookings), booking status retrieval, and public gallery access. Detail admin endpoints requiring JWT authentication including booking management (/api/admin/bookings), staff coordination, analytics reporting, and system administration. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error codes. Document the booking workflow API including inquiry submission, confirmation processing, status updates, and follow-up communications. Include detailed parameter specifications, validation rules, and response formats. Explain the admin authentication flow using JWT tokens and authorization patterns. Provide practical examples of API usage with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Document rate limiting, input validation, and security measures applied to each endpoint. Include troubleshooting guidance for common API integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed security architecture documentation for the Emerald system. Document the multi-layered security approach including JWT-based authentication for admin users, input sanitization and validation, rate limiting mechanisms, and CORS configuration. Explain the authentication flow including token generation, validation, and refresh strategies for admin dashboard access. Detail the authorization patterns including role-based access control and permission management. Document the data protection measures including environment variable management, API key security, and sensitive data handling. Detail the communication security including HTTPS enforcement, secure cookie handling, and cross-origin resource sharing policies. Document the threat mitigation strategies including DDoS protection, SQL injection prevention, and XSS attack prevention. wiki_catalogs.name: WhatsApp Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the WhatsApp integration system powered by Twilio. Document the Twilio WhatsApp API setup including sandbox environment configuration, production account setup, and message template approval processes. Explain the instant messaging workflows including client onboarding, booking confirmations, event reminders, and staff coordination messages. Detail the message formatting requirements, media attachment handling, and interactive message features. Provide examples of WhatsApp message flows including booking inquiries, confirmation responses, and event coordination. Document compliance requirements for WhatsApp business messaging, opt-in/opt-out handling, and privacy considerations. Include troubleshooting guides for common integration issues, rate limiting, and message formatting problems. Address cost optimization strategies and message volume management for high-traffic periods.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics & Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the analytics and reporting dashboard. Document the business metrics visualization including revenue tracking, booking volume trends, staff performance indicators, and client acquisition analytics. Explain the data aggregation processes, report generation workflows, and export capabilities. Detail the real-time dashboard widgets showing key performance indicators, conversion rates, and operational metrics. Document the custom report creation tools, date range filtering, and data export formats. Include the forecasting capabilities, trend analysis, and comparative reporting features. Address the underlying data models supporting analytics, caching strategies for performance, and real-time data synchronization mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema & Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Emerald Pearland Events database schema. Detail all entity relationships including Booking-Customer (one-to-many), Booking-Staff (many-to-many through assignments), and Admin-Bookings (administrative oversight). Document primary schemas: Booking with event details, customer relations, staff assignments, and status tracking; Customer with personal information and preferences; Staff with roles and availability; Admin with authentication credentials and permissions. Explain field definitions, data types, validation rules, and business constraints for each model. Include database schema diagrams showing entity relationships and foreign key constraints. Document indexing strategies, query patterns, and performance considerations. Specify data lifecycle including booking phases, customer data retention, and staff record management. Address data security measures, access control, and audit trails. Include sample data structures and common query examples demonstrating typical business operations.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for system settings and configuration management. wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Emerald Pearland Events admin dashboard system. Document the complete admin interface including booking management dashboard, staff coordination tools, client communication center, and analytics reporting. Detail the authentication system using JWT tokens with secure login, session management, and role-based access control. wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Emerald Pearland Events frontend application. Document the responsive website architecture including luxury event theme implementation, interactive booking form, and mobile-first design principles. Detail the mobile optimization features including touch-friendly interfaces, progressive web app capabilities, and adaptive layouts. Document the branding elements including custom styling, logo integration, and color scheme customization. Provide guidelines for maintaining responsive design across different devices and screen sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for the Emerald Pearland Events system. Document the production deployment using PM2 cluster mode including ecosystem configuration, process management, and auto-restart capabilities. Explain environment setup procedures for different deployment targets including development, staging, and production environments. Detail the health monitoring system including uptime checks, performance metrics, and alerting mechanisms. Document the maintenance procedures including database cleanup scripts, admin seeding processes, and system optimization tasks. wiki_catalogs.name: Security Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed security documentation for the Emerald Pearland Events system. Document the authentication and authorization implementation including JWT token-based admin authentication, password hashing with bcrypt, and session management. Explain the security middleware stack including helmet for security headers, express-rate-limit for protection against abuse, express-mongo-sanitize for input sanitization, and cors configuration for cross-origin requests. Detail the data protection measures including encryption at rest, secure transmission protocols, and sensitive data handling. Document the input validation and sanitization processes preventing injection attacks and malicious data entry. wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation and frequently asked questions for the Emerald Pearland Events system. Provide debugging tools and techniques for developers including error logging, API testing, and system monitoring approaches. Include escalation procedures for critical system issues and support contact information. Document preventive maintenance procedures and system health checks for proactive issue detection.

wiki_overview.content:
Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis of this project. Let me create the blog post.

<blog>

# Emerald Pearland Events - Premium Event Booking System

## Project Introduction

**Emerald Pearland Events** is a sophisticated, production-ready luxury event planning and booking system designed specifically for premium event services in Nairobi, Kenya. This comprehensive platform combines modern web technologies with robust backend infrastructure to deliver a seamless booking experience for luxury events including weddings, corporate events, and special occasions.

### Purpose Statement
The system serves as a complete digital solution for Emerald Pearland Events, managing client interactions, booking workflows, staff coordination, and automated communications while maintaining the highest standards of luxury and professionalism.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Streamlined Booking Process**: Provide an intuitive interface for clients to book premium events
- **Automated Workflow Management**: Handle complex booking lifecycle with automated notifications
- **Staff Coordination**: Facilitate efficient staff assignment and communication
- **Professional Branding**: Maintain luxury aesthetic throughout all touchpoints
- **Data-Driven Insights**: Track analytics and customer behavior for business optimization

### Target Audience
- **Primary**: Luxury event clients seeking premium booking experiences
- **Secondary**: Event planning staff and administrators
- **Tertiary**: Marketing and analytics teams for business insights

## Technical Architecture

The system follows a modern microservice-oriented architecture with clear separation between frontend presentation, backend APIs, and database operations.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Express.js Server]
    B --> C[MongoDB Database]
    B --> D[Email Service]
    B --> E[Cron Jobs]
    
    F[Admin Dashboard] --> B
    G[Mobile Clients] --> B
    
    D --> H[Brevo API]
    D --> I[Twilio API]
    E --> J[Node-Cron Scheduler]
    
    C --> K[Booking Models]
    C --> L[Customer Models]
    C --> M[Staff Models]
    C --> N[Analytics Models]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**
- Modern responsive design with cinematic animations
- GSAP-powered smooth scrolling and transitions
- Progressive Web App (PWA) capabilities
- Mobile-first responsive architecture

**Backend Layer**
- Express.js RESTful API with comprehensive middleware
- MongoDB with Mongoose ODM for data persistence
- Advanced security middleware (Helmet, rate limiting, sanitization)
- Real-time notifications via Web Push

**Service Layer**
- Automated email workflows with Brevo integration
- Staff notification system with push notifications
- Cron-based scheduled tasks for follow-ups and reminders
- WhatsApp integration for instant client communication

### Design Patterns

The system implements several key architectural patterns:

- **MVC Pattern**: Clear separation between models, views, and controllers
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted database operations
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic service creation for different notification types
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven notification system
- **Singleton Pattern**: Email service initialization

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Production Server** (`server-prod.js`)
- Central application entry point with comprehensive middleware stack
- Production-optimized configuration with security enhancements
- Cluster mode support for high availability

**Development Server** (`server.js`)
- Development environment with hot reloading capabilities
- Enhanced logging and debugging features

### Core Modules

**Booking Management** (`server/routes/bookingRoutes.js`)
- Comprehensive booking workflow handling
- Input validation and sanitization
- Multi-stage email automation
- WhatsApp integration for instant client communication

**Admin Interface** (`admin/`)
- Complete administrative dashboard
- User management and permissions
- Booking tracking and status updates
- Analytics and reporting

**Data Models** (`server/models/`)
- Mongoose schemas for all business entities
- Complex relationships between bookings, customers, and staff
- Automatic indexing for optimal query performance

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Variables** (`/.env`)
- Secure credential management
- Multiple environment configurations
- API key management for third-party services

**Process Management** (`ecosystem.config.js`)
- PM2 cluster configuration for production deployment
- Environment-specific settings
- Process monitoring and restart capabilities

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies**:
- **Express.js**: Web application framework
- **MongoDB/Mongoose**: Database and ODM
- **Bcrypt**: Password hashing and security
- **Helmet**: Security headers protection
- **Node-cron**: Scheduled task automation

**Communication Services**:
- **Brevo (Sendinblue)**: Transactional email delivery
- **Twilio**: SMS and WhatsApp messaging
- **Web Push**: Browser notification system

### Integration Points

**Third-Party Integrations**:
- **Google Analytics 4**: Performance and user behavior tracking
- **WhatsApp Business API**: Instant client communication
- **Brevo Email API**: Professional transactional emails
- **Google Fonts**: Typography enhancement

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant API
    participant EmailService
    participant Database
    participant WhatsApp
    
    Client->>API: Submit Booking Form
    API->>Database: Create Booking Record
    API->>EmailService: Send Confirmation Email
    API->>WhatsApp: Generate WhatsApp Link
    EmailService-->>Client: Booking Confirmation
    API-->>Client: Success Response
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Booking Management System**
- Multi-step booking workflow with validation
- Customer lifecycle tracking
- Automated status updates and notifications
- Real-time booking reference generation

**Client Communication Hub**
- Instant booking confirmation via email
- Personalized follow-up communications
- Event reminders and updates
- WhatsApp integration for direct messaging

**Staff Coordination Platform**
- Automated staff assignment notifications
- 48-hour pre-event alerts
- Supervisor and team member communication
- Performance tracking and feedback collection

**Administrative Dashboard**
- Real-time booking monitoring
- Customer relationship management
- Staff scheduling and assignments
- Analytics and reporting capabilities

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced Email Automation** (`server/services/emailService.js`)
- Seven distinct email templates for different scenarios
- Brevo API integration for reliable delivery
- HTML email templates with custom styling
- Reply-to functionality for seamless communication

**Cron Job Management** (`server/services/cronService.js`)
- Automated follow-up email system
- Event reminder notifications
- Staff alert system for upcoming events
- Configurable scheduling intervals

**Security and Performance**
- Comprehensive input sanitization
- Rate limiting for spam protection
- HTTPS enforcement and security headers
- Gzip compression for optimal loading

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> NewBooking: Client Submits Form
    NewBooking --> Contacted: Admin Review
    Contacted --> Confirmed: Client Agreement
    Confirmed --> Completed: Event Execution
    Confirmed --> Cancelled: Client Cancellation
    
    NewBooking --> FollowUp: Automated Email
    FollowUp --> Contacted: Client Response
    Contacted --> Confirmed: Payment & Details
```

**Real-Time Notifications**
- Push notification system for admin devices
- WhatsApp integration for instant client communication
- Email delivery tracking and monitoring
- Multi-channel communication strategy

**Analytics and Insights**
- Event tracking and conversion metrics
- Customer behavior analysis
- Staff performance monitoring
- Revenue and booking trend analysis

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [.env](.env)
- [server-prod.js](server-prod.js)
- [bookingRoutes.js](server/routes/bookingRoutes.js)
- [Booking.js](server/models/Booking.js)
- [emailService.js](server/services/emailService.js)
- [index.html](index.html)
- [cronService.js](server/services/cronService.js)
- [ecosystem.config.js](ecosystem.config.js)

</blog>
